title: rubus hirtus var. brachyandrus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. brachyandrus, also known as a thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its high content of salicylic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of thimbleberry include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting cardiovascular health. Thimbleberry can be consumed as a tea, infused in water, or dried to make a powder for use in herbal preparations, such as salves, tinctures, and ointments.
